meta.problem: An independent underwriter that assesses my live on-chain CFO agent (YIELD) at arm's length and prices insurance for it — bounded ⇒ insurable ⇒ scalable.

agent.name: the-underwriter
agent.model_rationale: Newest Opus-class — pricing risk correctly requires careful, disciplined reasoning, and a daily run still costs cents.
agent.system_summary: Fetches YIELD CFO's public dashboard + cross-checks the on-chain AgentMandate contract on Arc testnet, computes risk stats and a disclosed stub-v0 premium, writes a certificate + memo, and keeps a memory-store trend across runs.
never_dos: Never send a transaction, sign anything, or use any key/credential — HTTP GET and eth_call only
Never label a check FAILED or treat a data gap (null field, RPC timeout, decode error) as a mandate violation — checks are strictly PASS/PENDING/UNVERIFIED
Never call web_fetch/web_search (bypass the environment's network allowlist) — bash/curl/node only
Never present a certificate as a final bound insurance decision — always preliminary

outcome.description: Run today's underwriting assessment of the YIELD CFO agent; write certificate.json + memo.md; update the underwriting-history memory store.
rubric_criteria: certificate.json written with every required field, real values, no placeholders
every checks[].status is strictly PASS | PENDING | UNVERIFIED
no data gap is framed as a violation
premium.formula_version is stub-v0 and inputs are disclosed
memo.md exists as a ~1-page human-readable summary with the preliminary disclaimer
trend reflects the memory store and the store is updated with this run

tools.notes: web_fetch/web_search disabled on purpose — those tools have their own domain policy independent of the environment's networking allowlist, so disabling them keeps the agent's network access strictly confined to the 2 allowed hosts.

connections.notes: No connector, no vault, zero credentials by design — every data source is public read-only HTTP/RPC. Bind-coverage (a real write-action) is Next direction v2, gated always_ask.

stores.name: underwriting-history
stores.purpose: Every run's certificate + a running trend.md, so premiums can be compared assessment-to-assessment.
stores.seed: memory_seed/seed-assessment.json + memory_seed/trend.md — a synthetic, clearly-labeled 'seeded': true baseline record (1 compliant day, higher hazard) so run 1 can already state a trend.

schedule.kickoff: user.define_outcome (task + rubric above) — also available on demand via LAUNCH.md and via a manual deployment run

evals.notes: Brand-new agent, no past cases. Case 1 = today's first verified certificate, saved as evals/case-01/expected.* at close. Future agent versions re-run this before promoting to the deployment.

defaults_applied.write_actions: None at all — strictly read-only data sources, drafts-only output

interface.how: Certificate + memo land as files in session outputs; Console for run history and traces; LAUNCH.md is the terminal interface.

next_directions.why_deferred: Blocked on an external package that doesn't exist yet — not a credential gap, not a CMA limitation
next_directions.how: Already wired in the system prompt; the next run after the package publishes flips verification.mode automatically, zero code change

next_directions.what: Bind-coverage write-action behind an always_ask gate
next_directions.why_deferred: Out of scope for today's demo — no binding mechanism defined yet, certificates are explicitly preliminary
next_directions.how: Add a custom tool (or MCP connector once a target exists) with permission_policy: always_ask; confirm via user.tool_confirmation

next_directions.what: ERC-8004 registry cross-check as its own check
next_directions.why_deferred: Registry address + agentId given, but not which getters/ABI to call
next_directions.how: Confirm the registry ABI, add it alongside the AgentMandate ABI, add one more checks[] entry

next_directions.what: Re-run evals/ before promoting any new agent version to the deployment
next_directions.why_deferred: Process habit, not a build task
next_directions.how: evals/run-evals.sh against the new version; only update deployment.json's pinned version when verdicts hold

artifacts.v1_flip_proof: proof/simulate-verifier.sh — offline: flips a real certificate to machine-verified using a simulated verifier payload. Live path already wired in the system prompt.
artifacts.v2_bind_flow: bind/ — on-demand, human-gated bind-coverage via a SESSION-LOCAL custom tool (no new agent version; daily assessor untouched). Demo bind: policy POL-d2fa68 against uw-2026-07-22-42569c04.
artifacts.results_viewer: viewer/build-viewer.sh → viewer/results-viewer.html — self-contained premium-trend page (data baked in, opens offline).
artifacts.regression_verified: Live agent still v1, base toolset only, deployment references latest — bind flow never modified the scheduled assessor.
